title: Research paragraph missing source trail
purpose: Give a learner a low-conflict repair path when submitted work lacks enough visible thinking evidence.
review_cadence: Per assignment or after a weak-evidence submission.

missing_evidence.gap: Which source supports the main claim is unclear
Student reasoning between quote and conclusion is not visible
AI-use disclosure says "grammar only," but revision trail is absent

repair_requests.request: Explain the paragraph claim aloud or in 5-7 sentences in your own words
Mark which sentence is supported by which source note
Submit a short revision note naming any AI help and what you accepted/rejected

acceptable_repair_outcomes.outcome: Evidence now supports the claim and learner can explain it
Teacher schedules a short conference if evidence remains unclear

sections.heading: Missing evidence
items: Which source supports the main claim is unclear
Student reasoning between quote and conclusion is not visible
AI-use disclosure says “grammar only,” but revision trail is absent

sections.heading: Repair request
items: Explain the paragraph claim aloud or in 5–7 sentences in your own words
Mark which sentence is supported by which source note
Submit a short revision note naming any AI help and what you accepted/rejected

sections.heading: Acceptable repair outcomes
items: Evidence now supports the claim and learner can explain it
Teacher schedules a short conference if evidence remains unclear

review_flags: Do not use as detector evidence
Use local educator judgment for grading/conduct decisions
boundary_note: Review aid only. This is not forensic proof, misconduct determination, or detector validation. It is a learning-evidence repair workflow.
notes: Synthetic example aligned with “The Next Classroom May Be a Folder.”
